title: Smoky Saudi Arabian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Saudi Arabian dish featuring tender lamb, cranberries, and a hint of smokiness, all wrapped up in a silky and buttery sauce. This monsoon-inspired recipe is perfect for a cozy dinner on a rainy night.
instructions: Heat the butter in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the lamb and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the cranberries and chicken broth, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 1.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
